title: Đề luyện tập 02 - RISC-V, Cache và Pipeline
description: Đề số 02 được tách riêng trong exams/exam_02.js. Mỗi câu 10 điểm, tổng 100 điểm. Đáp án chỉ hiện sau khi nộp bài.

questions.title: Câu 1 - 2-way set associative cache, byte addressing
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
2-Way Set Associative Cache
Write policy: Write-back using write allocate (one Dirty bit for a block)
Block size is 32 bytes
Cache size is 64 KB
Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses.

Fill in the following values.

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 64KB / 32B = 2048. Sets = 2048 / 2 = 1024 => set index = 10 bits. Offset = log2(32) = 5 bits. Tag = 32 - 10 - 5 = 17 bits. Total bits = 2048 × (32×8 data + 17 tag + 1 valid + 1 dirty) = 563,200 bits.

questions.title: Câu 2 - 4-way set associative cache, byte addressing
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
4-Way Set Associative Cache
Write policy: Write-back using write allocate (one Dirty bit for a block)
Block size is 64 bytes
Cache size is 32 KB
Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses.

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 32KB / 64B = 512. Sets = 512 / 4 = 128 => index = 7 bits. Offset = 6 bits. Tag = 32 - 7 - 6 = 19 bits. Total = 512 × (64×8 + 19 + valid 1 + dirty 1) = 272,896 bits.

questions.title: Câu 3 - Fully associative cache parameters
questions.prompt: Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with two-word blocks and a total size of 8 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

fields.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Cache blocks = 8/2 = 4. Fully associative => 1 set and 0 index bits. Memory blocks = 2^8/2 = 128. Offset = log2(2) = 1 bit, so tag = 8 - 1 = 7 bits. Total = 4 × (2×32 data + 7 tag + 1 valid) = 288 bits.

questions.title: Câu 4 - Fully associative cache với true LRU
questions.prompt:
Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with two-word blocks and a total size of 8 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x03, 0x04, 0x10, 0x20, 0x03, 0x24, 0x05, 0x21, 0x30, 0x04, 0x25, 0x31, 0x40, 0x03

Assume a true LRU replacement policy. For each reference, identify the binary word address, the tag, and hit/miss.

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Block size = 2 words, so the last bit is offset and tag = address >> 1. The cache holds 4 blocks. Hit/miss sequence: M, M, M, M, H, M, M, H, M, H, H, H, M, M. The cache is full and there are 5 replacements.

questions.title: Câu 5 - 3-way set associative cache, điền Way theo từng dòng
questions.prompt:
Assume the memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a 3-way set associative cache with 2-word blocks and a total size of 48 words. Assume 1 word = 64 bits.

First, fill in the cache parameters. Then use true LRU. For each reference, identify binary address, tag, set index, offset, hit/miss, and the tags in each way after the reference has been handled.

Lưu ý: Nếu một Way có nhiều dòng như T(index)=tag, T(xxx)=yyyy, mỗi dòng sẽ là một ô nhập riêng. Các dòng trong Way nên xếp theo set index tăng dần.

fields_intro.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Cache blocks = 48/2 = 24. Because this is 3-way, sets = 24/3 = 8, so index = 3 bits. Offset = log2(2) = 1 bit. Tag = 8 - 3 - 1 = 4 bits. Total bits = 24 × (2×64 data + 4 tag + 1 valid) = 3192 bits. Replacements occur only in set 111: 0x2e replaces T(111)=0000 in Way 1, and 0xce replaces T(111)=0001 in Way 2.

questions.title: Câu 6 - Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: sw
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with the simple instruction set: add, sub, and, or, lw, sw, beq.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
sw x13, 160(x9)
at address 4096. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 32i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 500.

Fill out the control signals and signals at locations A, B, C, ..., R.
questions.solution: For sw, RegWrite = 0, MemWrite = 1, MemRead = 0, ALUSrc = 1. rs1 = x9 = 288, rs2 = x13 = 416, imm = 160. Effective address = 288 + 160 = 448. The branch adder still computes PC + (imm << 1) = 4096 + 320 = 4416, even though Branch = 0.

questions.title: Câu 7 - Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: lw
questions.prompt:
Consider the same single-cycle RISC-V processor.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
lw x18, 96(x11)
at address 4100. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 32i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 500.
questions.solution: lw x18, 96(x11): x11 = 32×11 = 352, effective address = 352 + 96 = 448, memory data = 448 + 500 = 948. Instruction = 0x0605A903. For I-type lw, bits [24:20] are imm[4:0], not rs2; 96 = 000001100000, so D = 00000 and the register file second read value L = x0 = 0. RegWrite=1, ALUSrc=1, MemRead=1, MemWrite=0, MemtoReg=1.

questions.title: Câu 8 - Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: sub
questions.prompt:
Consider the same single-cycle RISC-V processor.

Let the processor execute the instruction:
sub x20, x12, x10
at address 4104. Suppose the initial value of register xi is 32i for i = 1,...,31. Suppose the initial value of memory location with address M is M + 500.
questions.solution: For sub, ALUSrc = 0, MemtoReg = 0, RegWrite = 1, ALUOp = 10, and ALU control O = 0110. x12 = 384 and x10 = 320, so the ALU result is 64. Data memory is not read, so M is unknown (?) and N = 64.

questions.title: Câu 9 - 5-stage pipeline with forwarding and hazard detection
questions.prompt:
Assume that the following code sequence is executed on the 5-stage pipelined processor with forwarding and hazard detection units shown below:
add x6, x1, x2
lw  x7, 4(x6)
lw  x8, 0(x6)
or  x7, x6, x7
sw  x7, 0(x6)
For the first seven cycles, specify the values of control signals at A, B, C, D, E, F, and G.
questions.solution: This sequence has the same dependency pattern as the standard exercise. Correct rows: 1st/2nd = 1 1 xx xx 1 x x; 3rd = 1 1 00 00 1 x x; 4th = 1 1 10 00 1 1 x; 5th = 1 1 01 00 1 1 1; 6th = 1 1 00 01 1 1 1; 7th = 1 1 00 10 1 1 1.

questions.title: Câu 10 - Execution time: single-cycle vs pipeline
questions.prompt:
Consider the following sequence of 32-bit RISC-V instructions:
add x8,  x1,  x2
lw  x9,  0(x8)
add x10, x9,  x3
sub x11, x10, x8
sw  x11, 4(x8)
a) Single-cycle processor clock cycle time = 1000 ps.
b) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, without forwarding or hazard detection units. Insert NOPs to ensure correct execution.
c) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, with forwarding/hazard detection units.
d) Which case gives the medium performance?

fields.label: a) Execution time on single-cycle processor
b) Number of cycles after inserting NOPs
b) Execution time without forwarding/hazard detection
c) Number of stalls/NOPs needed with forwarding/hazard detection
c) Execution time with forwarding/hazard detection

questions.solution: Single-cycle: 5 × 1000 = 5000 ps. Without forwarding/hazard detection, dependencies add→lw, lw→add, add→sub, and sub→sw each need 2 NOPs, so 8 NOPs total. There are 13 instructions including NOPs, so pipeline cycles = 13 + 4 = 17 and time = 4250 ps. With forwarding/hazard detection, only the adjacent load-use lw x9 → add x10 needs 1 stall, so cycles = 5 + 4 + 1 = 10 and time = 2500 ps. Medium performance is case b.
